---
title: Publications
---

Papers with an asterisk (^\*^) denote equal contribution. The canonical BibTeX record for all papers is maintained in [`pubs.bib`](pubs.bib).

---

## Mathematical Ecology

**K. Shen, S. A. Levin and D. D. Patterson** (2026).
Spatial modeling of forest-savanna bistability: impacts of fire dynamics and timescale separation.
*Journal of Mathematical Biology*, 92, 44.
[[Open Access](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00285-026-02363-9)]

**L. Xu, D. D. Patterson, S. A. Levin and J. Wang** (2025).
Global stability and tipping point prediction of the coral reef ecosystem.
*Earth System Dynamics*, 16, 1503.
[[Open Access](https://esd.copernicus.org/articles/16/1503/2025/)]

**J. D. Touboul, J. Li, D. D. Patterson and S. A. Levin** (2025).
New challenges in spatial ecology.
*Japan Journal of Industrial and Applied Mathematics*.
[[Open Access](https://doi.org/10.1007/s13160-025-00720-7)]

**J. Siu, W. Wu, D. D. Patterson, S. A. Levin and J. Wang** (2025).
Revealing physical mechanisms of pattern formation and switching in ecosystems via landscape and flux.
*Advanced Science*, 2501776.
[[Open Access](https://doi.org/10.1002/advs.202501776)]

**D. D. Patterson, S. A. Levin, A. C. Staver and J. D. Touboul** (2024).
Pattern formation in mesic savannas.
*Bulletin of Mathematical Biology*, 86(3).
[[Open Access](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11538-023-01231-7)]

**D. D. Patterson, A. C. Staver, S. A. Levin and J. D. Touboul** (2023).
Spatial dynamics with heterogeneity.
*SIAM Journal on Applied Mathematics*, S225–S248.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.05007)]

**L. Xu, D. D. Patterson, S. A. Levin and J. Wang** (2023).
Non-equilibrium early-warning signals for critical transitions in ecological systems.
*Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences*, 120(5), e2218663120.
[[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2218663120)]

**L. Xu, D. D. Patterson, A. C. Staver, S. A. Levin and J. Wang** (2021).
Unifying deterministic and stochastic ecological dynamics via a landscape-flux approach.
*Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences*, 118(24), e2103779118.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.08198)]

**D. D. Patterson, A. C. Staver, S. A. Levin and J. D. Touboul** (2020).
Probabilistic foundations of spatial mean-field models in ecology and applications.
*SIAM Journal on Applied Dynamical Systems*, 19(4), 2682–2719.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1911.06770)]

---

## Mathematical Epidemiology: Malaria

**D. D. Patterson, L. M. Childs, I. J. Stopard, N. Chitnis, S. Serrato-Arroyo and M. A. Greischar** (2025).
Immunity can impose a reproduction-survival tradeoff on human malaria parasites.
*Evolution*, qpaf238.
[[Open Access](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2025.01.27.635035v1)]

**Z. Qu^\*^, D. D. Patterson^\*^, L. Childs, C. Edholm, J. Ponce, O. Prosper and L. Zhao** (2025).
Mathematical modeling of malaria vaccination with seasonality and immune feedbacks.
*PLoS Computational Biology*, 21(5), e1012988.

**Z. Qu^\*^, D. D. Patterson^\*^, L. Childs, C. Edholm, J. Ponce, O. Prosper and L. Zhao** (2023).
Modeling immunity to malaria with an age-structured PDE framework.
*SIAM Journal on Applied Mathematics*, 83(3), 1098–1125.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.12721)]

---

## Mathematical Modelling: Developmental Biology

**J. Feng^\*^, W. H. Hsu^\*^, D. D. Patterson, C. S. Tseng, Z. H. Zhuang, H. W. Hsin, Y. T. Huang, A. Faedo, J. L. Rubenstein, J. D. Touboul and S. J. Chou** (2021).
COUP-TFI specifies the medial entorhinal cortex identity and induces differential cell adhesion to determine the integrity of its boundary with neocortex.
*Science Advances*, 7(27), eabf6808.
[[DOI](https://advances.sciencemag.org/content/7/27/eabf6808)]

---

## Functional Differential Equations

### Finite-time blow-up of systems with memory

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2018).
Blow-up and superexponential growth in superlinear Volterra equations.
*Discrete and Continuous Dynamical Systems (Series A)*, 38(8), 3993–4017.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1710.07583)]

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2017).
Growth rates of solutions of superlinear ordinary differential equations.
*Applied Mathematics Letters*, 71, 30–37.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1702.06427)]

### Growth rates of systems with memory

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2021).
Growth and fluctuation in perturbed nonlinear Volterra equations.
*Applied Mathematics and Computation*, 396, 125938.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1612.00515)]

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2018).
Growth rates of sublinear functional and Volterra differential equations.
*SIAM Journal on Mathematical Analysis*, 50(2), 2086–2110.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1602.06256)]

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2017).
Memory dependent growth in sublinear Volterra differential equations.
*Journal of Integral Equations and Applications*, 29(4), 531–584.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1604.00945)]

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2017).
Hartman-Wintner growth results for sublinear functional differential equations.
*Electronic Journal of Differential Equations*, 2017(21), 1–45.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1612.00512)]

### Discrete systems with memory

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2017).
Large fluctuations and growth rates of linear Volterra summation equations.
*Journal of Difference Equations and Applications*, 23(6), 1047–1080.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1610.02835)]

### Convergence rates of stable solutions

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2016).
Classification of convergence rates of solutions of perturbed ordinary differential equations with regularly varying nonlinearity.
*Electronic Journal of Qualitative Theory of Differential Equations*, Proceedings of the 10th Colloquium on the Qualitative Theory of Differential Equations, 3, 1–38.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1303.3345)]

**J. A. D. Appleby and D. D. Patterson** (2014).
On necessary and sufficient conditions for preserving convergence rates to equilibrium in deterministically and stochastically perturbed differential equations with regularly varying nonlinearity.
In *Recent Advances in Delay Differential and Difference Equations*, Springer Proceedings in Mathematics & Statistics 94, 1–85.
[[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.1024)]
